description: Add a simple "hello world" GET endpoint that returns a greeting message. This serves as the foundational route to verify the API is working end-to-end.

userStories.title: Write failing test for GET /hello endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a failing test for GET /hello so that I have a clear target for the implementation. Create tests/test_hello.py with a test that sends GET /hello and asserts 200 status with {"message": "Hello, world!"} response body.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/test_hello.py exists with an async test for GET /hello
Test asserts response status code is 200
Test asserts response body is {"message": "Hello, world!"}
Test fails when run (no implementation yet)
userStories.notes: Effort: S. TDD red phase. Completed — test fails with 404 as expected.

userStories.title: Implement GET /hello route
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the GET /hello endpoint implemented so that the failing test passes. Create app/routers/hello.py with a router, app/schemas/hello.py with the response schema, and register the router in app/main.py.

userStories.title: Verify endpoint with curl against running server
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify the endpoint works against a live server. Start the server and confirm curl http://localhost:<PORT>/hello returns the expected JSON response.
userStories.notes: Effort: S. Final verification — last in dependency chain.
